componentScope.label: ingredient embeddings
componentScope.guidance: Document the ingredient-embedding subsystem inside this FastAPI backend ONLY — embedding generation, storage, retrieval, similarity search, and any ingestion or refresh pipeline that feeds it. Treat the rest of the backend (auth, unrelated routes, generic CRUD, etc.) as out-of-scope. Locate the component by searching for: 'embedding', 'embed', 'vector', 'ingredient', 'similarity', 'cosine', 'pgvector', 'chroma', 'faiss', 'sentence-transformers', 'openai.Embedding', 'voyage', 'cohere'. The repo root contains app/, scripts/, alembic/, data/. Likely homes for the subsystem: app/<something with ingredient/embedding>, scripts/<embedding generation script>, alembic versions touching a vector column. Confirm location before scoping the page.

emphasis: the embedding model (provider, model id, dimension)
where embeddings are stored (table name, column type, index)
how new ingredients get embedded (batch script vs on-write trigger)
how queries work (similarity metric, top-k, filters)
any caching, batching, or rate-limit handling around the model API
schema-evidence pairs the validator can resolve to file:line
skip: unrelated FastAPI routes outside the ingredient-embedding flow
alembic migrations unrelated to the embeddings table or column
deploy scripts unless they materially affect the embedding subsystem

pages.purpose: Single agent-docs page documenting the ingredient-embedding subsystem inside backend-fastapi-copy. Must conform to docs/AGENT_DOCS_SCHEMA.md.

references.note: agent-docs schema produces structured data, not narrative prose. Calibrate against src/pages/agent-docs/search-trend-scraper.mdx and table-manager.mdx for shape. Component-scope means the page documents ONE subsystem of a larger codebase — the sourcePath should point at the most representative file (likely the embedding service module or generation script), and dependencies/mechanisms should describe only the embedding flow.

provisionalNote: Third pilot of the v2 /document-project pipeline; first component-scoped run. The docStyle block is included to satisfy devdeck-research-agent v1 which still expects a rich brief. Component scoping is described in componentScope; research-agent should respect that boundary and not document the entire backend.
